tei:elementSpec.tei:desc: A note represents a single musical note. Its xml:id identifies the note and interval.chromatic or interval.diatonic specifies its pitch interval alteration in (chromatic) halftone or diatonic steps.

tei:constraintSpec.tei:desc: Only one of the three pitch-specifying attributes midi.pitch, interval.chromatic, or interval.diatonic may be set on a single note element.

tei:constraint.schematron:assert: Only one of the attributes 'midi.pitch', 'interval.chromatic', or 'interval.diatonic' may be set on a note element.

tei:attDef.tei:desc: Specifies the chromatic pitch interval alteration of this note as a double value, e.g. 0.0 or -1.0.
Specifies the diatonic pitch interval alteration of this note as an integer value, e.g. 0 or -1.

tei:exemplum.tei:p: The first ornament applies two alterations (#n2, #n3) to the principal note (#princNote1) creating an upper turn. The second ornament defines a half tone trill ornament, starting with the auxiliary note (#n4) and landing on the principal note (#princNote2), with no repetition as the repetitions attribute is not specified and 0 by default.

tei:remarks.tei:p: Only one of the three pitch-specifying attributes can be set on a single note element. Whereby midi.pitch is the most explicit, interval.chromatic is the more general interval specification, and interval.diatonic is context-sensitive.
